calendar.matters:
Petition To Suspend Executor
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Verriere and Rebecca Eisenberg appeared by zoom for and with Curtis Smolar (Beneficiary) by zoom.
Michael Dobrov appeared by zoom for and with Scott Smolar (Executor/Petitioner) by zoom.
Robert Sprague (Litigation Counsel) appeared by zoom for and with Scott Smolar.

The Court noted that it received a number of things late yesterday and that it had no chance to review them. Mr. Sprague stated that he has not received answers to the interrogatories with respect to the litigation issue, that everything has been reduced to cash and that he contends that Scott has done nothing wrong. Mr. Verriere raised the issue of the advance payment of Mr. Sprague's attorney's fees without a court order, stated that his preference is that the court approve the accounting and surcharge Scott for those attorney's fees plus interest at 10%, and that removal is appropriate. Mr. Verriere also stated that Scott has shown failure to administer the estate properly, that he has made misrepresentations to the court, that Scott failed to disclose transactions between him and his mother, and that removal is appropriate because of a poorly conflicted administration. Discussion was held as to how the court could charge interest, whether or not a surcharge is appropriate, whether or not the fees paid to Mr. Sprague were to defend the estate and who his representation benefitted, whether or not said fees were permissive under the probate code, whether Mr. Sprague should be permitted to submit billing statements, whether a request for extraordinary fees is untimely after a petition for final distribution, etc. The court passed the matter and encouraged the parties to talk. The matter was recalled with all parties present. Mr. Verriere informed the court that Scott agreed that Curtis' distribution will be increased by the amount of the attorney's fees that were paid to Mr. Sprague, that Curtis agreed to waive the interest on those fees, and that distribution is to be made within five days of the order. Mr. Verriere also clarified that Curtis will receive $205,632.38 and that Scott will receive $140,599.58. The Petition to Suspend Executor, and the Petition to Remove Executor are off calendar. The First and Final Account and Report of Executor and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
